excerpt.as_written: في الابتدآ صنع الله السمّاء والارض. انهُ قد يليق اولاً بالعتيد ان يخبّر بشي في خليقه العالم
translation: In the beginning, God made the heavens and the earth. It is fitting first of all to say something about the creation of the world…

excerpt.as_written: ليفرح ذو العباده الحسنه براي الحق والمجّد الرب الذي له المجد والعزّ الي ابد الادهار امين
translation: And let the one who possesses virtuous devotion rejoice with the mind of truth, glorifying the Lord to Whom be the glory and power forever. Amen

contents.label: First treatise, ff. 008r - 023r|~Second treatise, ff. 023v - 038r|~Third treatise, ff. 38r - 053r|~Fourth treatise, ff. 053r - 063v

work.desc_title: Gregory of Nyssa, On the Creation of Humanity

excerpt.as_written: لو كان يمكن ان يُكرّم اولوا الفضيله الزايدون علي غيرهم فيها بكرامات المال
translation: If it were possible to honor those who had virtue which exceeded others with the honor of money…

excerpt.as_written: لما قال لنصنع الأنسان علي صورتنا وشبهنا الذي له المجد والعزّ والاكرام الآن وكل اوان والي دهر الداهرين امين
translation: ...when he said, “Let us make humanity in our image and likeness,” to whom be the glory, power, and honor always and forever. Amen

note: This text is divided into thirty-one chapters

excerpt.as_written: ربّنا يسوع المسيح امر قايلاً الذي تقولونه في الظلمه قولوه في النور
translation: Our Lord Jesus Christ commanded, saying, “Whatever you say in darkness, say also in the light”...

excerpt.as_written: قد خل الي الحياه الدايمه ونرث ملكوت السموات كما وعدنا الاهنا يسوع المسيح الذي له المجد الي الابد امين
translation: He was exalted to eternal life that we might inherit the heavenly kingdom, as our Lord Jesus Christ promised us, to whom be the glory forever. Amen

note: The rubric indicates that perhaps this is a portion of Basil’s work. There are several rubricized sections throughout with translations of the rubrics in Greek in the margins for certain sections (e.g., f. 248r, 252v, etc.).

excerpt.as_written: لمّا اخترت لك ايها الأخ المحب فلسفة المسيح سيرة الرهبنه ورفعت عقلك عن جميع الشهوات واللذات والهموم العالميه
translation: When you, oh beloved brother, chose the philosophy of Christ, the monastic life, and freed your mind from all passions, pleasures, and worldly cares…

excerpt.as_written: ونطرد عدوّنا ويعطينا سبَّبا لهدوّ افكارنا لنعبدهُ بفرح دٍايم الذي يجب لهُ المجد الي الابد امين
translation: …and we drive out our enemy, and he provides us the cause by which we guide our thoughts rightly to his worship with everlasting joy, to Whom be the glory for every. Amen

note: The text begins with a prologue not found in the Greek text (cf. CPG 2877). There are several rubricized sections throughout with translations of the rubrics in Greek in the margins for certain sections (e.g., f. 307r, 313v, etc.).

work_wit.alt_title: Basil of Caesarea, Ascetica with prologue

excerpt.as_written: سيرة النساك قصدها واحد وهو خلاص النفس
translation: The life of the ascetic has only one purpose which is the salvation of the soul…

excerpt.as_written: فمن يتمسّك بهذا الشوق في الله فهو لا يتفرغ ان يهتم بحوايج الجسد
translation: …so, whoever adheres to this longing for God is not free to care for the needs of the body

note: There are several rubricized sections throughout with translations of the rubrics in Greek in the margins for certain sections (e.g., f. 323r, 326v, etc.). The prologue is a separate text in the CPG (cf. CPG 2883).

excerpt.as_written: ان البنّاؤون اذا مَا أرادوا ان يرفعوا اعظم اعمال الى العلو
translation: If the builders want to raise up the greatest works to the heights…

excerpt.as_written: فيمنحنا الرب ثواب المقولات واياكم ثمر ما سمعتموه بنعمة مسيحه الذي له المَجد والقُدرة في كُلّ الادهار امين
translation: …so, the Lord has given the reward of those things which were said, and you are the fruit of what things you have heard, by the grace of Christ to Whom be the glory and power in all ages. Amen

note: The text contains some rubricized sections typical of commentary, including quotes of the Psalm in Arabic and questions and answers (e.g. f. 399v)

work.desc_title: Basil of Caesarea, Homily on Deuteronomy 15.9
creator: ark:/21198/s1101c

excerpt.as_written: ان موهبة النطق واستعما لها ممّا وهبه لنا الله خالقنا لكي
translation: Both the gift of speech and its use are what God, our Creator, has given us so that…

excerpt.as_written: فقول مع النبي لقد عجبت معرفتك مني فاصغي الي ذاتك حتي تصغي الي الله الذي له المجد والعز والاكرام والسجود والقدره الان وكل اوان والي اباد الدهور امين
translation: …then, say as with the prophet, “Your knowledge of me is marvelous,” and heed that one as you heed God, to Whom be the glory, power, honor, worship, and might, now and always, to the end of the ages. Amen

work_wit.alt_title: Basil of Caesarea, Sermo xi (Sermo asceticus et exhoratio de renuntiatione mundi)

excerpt.as_written: قال الصوت الآلهي تعالوا الي يا معشر المتعوبين والموقرين وانا اريحكم
translation: The divine voice said, “Come to me, oh you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest"

excerpt.as_written: فستدخل بنعمة الله الي مُلك السمايها المُصباح مبتهجاً مع المسيح الي دهر الدهور امين
translation: …then, you will enter by the grace of God into the heavenly kingdom of the morning, rejoicing with Christ to the age of ages. Amen

work.desc_title: Basil of Caesarea, On the Separation of the Soul from the Body
creator: ark:/21198/s1101c

excerpt.as_written: ليس يفزّع السيف ولا الرمح كما تفزّع ساعة الموت للخاطي
translation: Neither sword nor spear terrify as much as the hour of death terrifies the sinner…

excerpt.as_written: لان الله عز اسمه لمثل هولاء النساك يحب ويقبل وديعين وصالح و متحتين الذين ما يخرج من افواههم عش والله السبح دايما
translation: Because God exalts the name of those ascetics who love and accept the meek, the righteous, and the humble for whom whatever comes out of their mouth is life. To God be the glory always

note: On f. 442v, there is a rubric stating that the following portion of text is from the Ascetica of Basil

excerpt.as_written: ينبغي للنفس البثول ان تجعل هواها كُلّه خادمه لختنها
translation: The soul of the virgin ought to make all its desires the servant to its bridegroom…

excerpt.as_written: ويدخل علينا اللصّ وياخد مَا في بيوتنا ولله الرب المسيح التسبحه والمجد والشُكر والوقار الي دهر الداهرين امين
translation: …and may the thief enter us and take what is in our homes. To God, Lord Jesus Christ, be the glory, praise, thanks, and reverence to the age of ages. Amen

note: On f. 447r, the rubric states that the following text comes from Basil on the life of monks and their services

work.desc_title: John Chrysostom, On the Fear of God preached by the Clergy
creator: ark:/21198/s1x303

excerpt.as_written: تعليم فاضل يعّلمنا اليوم داوود النبي حَيث يقول
translation: Virtuous teaching informs us today, where the prophet David says…

excerpt.as_written: هذا التعليم هو يهدي الي السما وتجعل الانسان لا يقع من ذلك السُبح ويقربه الي المسيح الذي له المجد والاكرام والسجود مع الاب الذي لا ابتداءً له والروح القدس المحيي الان ودايماً والي اباد الدهور امين
translation: This teaching is a guide to heaven and causes humanity not to fall from that glory and brings him closer to Christ, to Whom be the glory, etc.

cataloguer.message: Texts identified and described as part of the NEH Sinai Data Portal project (2021-2024).
